introSection: Creepy Crawler is a steel roller coaster currently standing but not operating at Oakwood Theme Park in Narberth, Pembrokeshire, Wales, UK.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster was new to Camelot Theme Park in 1995 as The Sorcerer. It opened alongside Excailbur. It was removed after the 1997 season and was replaced by The Quintain the following year.

It was then sold and relocated to M&D's Scotland's Theme Park in Motherwell, Scotland, where it reopened in 1998. While at M&D's, the coaster went under various names, such as Cobra, Express and Space Coaster. After it left M&D's after the 2015 season, multiple rumours stated that the ride was sold to Oakwood Theme Park in Narberth, Pembrokeshire, Wales. These rumours were confirmed when the coaster was seen under construction at the park.

On 12 August 2016, the ride was confirmed to be part of a James and the Giant Peach area in Oakwood's summer leaflet. The park later confirmed on their Facebook page that the coaster itself would be named Flight of the Giant Peach. The ride opened on 1 August 2017 and was situated within the new Dahl Land area. Riders passed through the "Aunt Sponge and Aunt Spiker’s House" walk-through attraction prior to boarding the roller coaster.

For the 2018 season, the theme was removed and the coaster became known as Creepy Crawler. This was due to the surrounding area becoming Spooky Street, due to the Roald Dahl theming being removed. The area also contains Witches' Brew.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The roller coaster has blue track and dark blue supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Riders are held in by over-the-shoulder restraints. For its reopening at Oakwood, the trains were refurbished with one given a ladybird theme and the other a grasshopper theme.

fullText:
Creepy Crawler is a steel roller coaster currently standing but not operating at Oakwood Theme Park in Narberth, Pembrokeshire, Wales, UK.

The roller coaster was new to Camelot Theme Park in 1995 as The Sorcerer. It opened alongside Excailbur. It was removed after the 1997 season and was replaced by The Quintain the following year.

It was then sold and relocated to M&D's Scotland's Theme Park in Motherwell, Scotland, where it reopened in 1998. While at M&D's, the coaster went under various names, such as Cobra, Express and Space Coaster. After it left M&D's after the 2015 season, multiple rumours stated that the ride was sold to Oakwood Theme Park in Narberth, Pembrokeshire, Wales. These rumours were confirmed when the coaster was seen under construction at the park.

On 12 August 2016, the ride was confirmed to be part of a James and the Giant Peach area in Oakwood's summer leaflet. The park later confirmed on their Facebook page that the coaster itself would be named Flight of the Giant Peach. The ride opened on 1 August 2017 and was situated within the new Dahl Land area. Riders passed through the "Aunt Sponge and Aunt Spiker’s House" walk-through attraction prior to boarding the roller coaster.

For the 2018 season, the theme was removed and the coaster became known as Creepy Crawler. This was due to the surrounding area becoming Spooky Street, due to the Roald Dahl theming being removed. The area also contains Witches' Brew.

The roller coaster has blue track and dark blue supports.

Riders are held in by over-the-shoulder restraints. For its reopening at Oakwood, the trains were refurbished with one given a ladybird theme and the other a grasshopper theme.
